title: Roundabout (robo) receptor inhibitors and uses thereof
abstract: The present invention, in some embodiments thereof, relates to inhibitory compositions binding to the ectodomain of a Roundabout (Robo) receptor and to the use of same for downregulating Robo-mediated signaling. In particular, the present invention provides moieties that bind to and prevent dimerization of an Ig-like Robo receptor ectodomain, thereby inhibiting Robo receptor activity.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An agent capable of specifically binding to a Robo receptor ectodomain polypeptide selected from the group consisting of a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 3 (D3), a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 4 (D4), a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin- like domain 5 (D5), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 1 (FnIII-l)(D6), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 2 (FnIII-2)(D7), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 3 (Fnlll- 3)(D8), a Robo receptor D3-D4 hinge region, a Robo receptor D4-D5 hinge region, a Robo receptor D5-D6 hinge region, a Robo receptor D6-D7 hinge region and a Robo receptor D7-D8 hinge region, or a portion thereof, and inhibiting Robo receptor dimerization. 2. The agent of claim 1 , comprising a moiety which specifically binds a Robo receptor ectodomain polypeptide selected from the group consisting of Robo receptor Immunoglobulin- like domain 3 (D3), a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 4 (D4), a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 5 (D5), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 1 (Fnlll- 1)(D6), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 2 (FnIII-2)(D7) and a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 3 (FnIII-3)(D8). 3. The agent of claim 1, wherein said agent does not bind any of Robo receptor Dl or D2. 4. The agent of claim 1 , further capable of inhibiting homo-dimerization of an isolated Robo receptor D4-D5 polypeptide. 5. The agent of claim 1 , further capable of inhibiting homo-dimerization of an isolated Robo receptor D4 polypeptide. 6. The agent of claim 1 , further capable of inhibiting homo-dimerization of an isolated Robo receptor D1-D3 polypeptide. 7. The agent of claim 1 , further capable of inhibiting homo-dimerization of an isolated Robo receptor D3 polypeptide. 8. The agent of any one of claims 1-7, wherein said binding is characterized by a micromolar or sub-micromolar dissociation constant (K D ). 9. The agent of claim 8, wherein said binding is characterized by a dissociation constant in the range of 10nM-5000 nM. 10. The agent of claim 8, wherein said binding is characterized by a dissociation constant in the range of 10-250 nM. 11. The agent of claim 1, comprising a moiety binding to Robo receptor ectodomain D4-D4 dimerization interface. 12. The agent of claim 11, wherein said moiety binds to ectodomain D4-D4 dimerization interface or a portion thereof, wherein said binding inhibits Robo receptor D4-D4 dimerization and wherein said agent does not bind either of Robo receptor Dl or D2 or D1-D2 hinge region. 13. The agent of claim 11, wherein said moiety binds to a Robo sequence selected from the group consisting of Robo 2(336-340), Robo 2(369-377), Robo 2(382-390), Robo 2(402-410) of human Robo 2 or the Robo receptor ectodomain D4 dimerization interface of Robo, wherein said binding inhibits Robo receptor D4-D4 dimerization. 14. The agent of claim 11, wherein said Robo receptor ectodomain D4 dimerization interface comprises the amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 34. 15. The agent of claim 11, wherein said Robo receptor ectodomain D4 dimerization interface comprises the amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 31. 16. The agent of claim 1, comprising a moiety binding to Robo receptor ectodomain D3-D3 dimerization interface. 17. The agent of claim 16, wherein said moiety binds to ectodomain D3-D3 dimerization interface or a portion thereof, wherein said binding inhibits Robo receptor D3-D3 

 dimerization and wherein said agent does not bind either of Robo receptor Dl or D2 or D1-D2 hinge region. 18. The agent of claim 16, wherein said moiety binds to Robo sequence selected from the group consisting of Robo 2(259-KKDDADLPRGRY SEQ ID NO: 18), Robo 1(296- RKDDGALPKSRY SEQ ID NO: 19), Robo 3(292-RKEDGELPTGRY, SEQ ID NO: 20) of human Robo or the Robo receptor ectodomain D3 dimerization interface of Robo, wherein said binding inhibits Robo receptor D3-D3 dimerization. 19. The agent of claim 16, wherein said Robo receptor ectodomain D3 dimerization interface comprises the amino acid sequence as set forth in SEQ ID NO: 18. 20. The agent of any one of claims 6-10 and 16-19, wherein said binding to said Robo receptor ectodomain D3 inhibits intracellular signaling via Robo receptor intracellular domain. 21. The agent of any one of claims 1-20, wherein said binding to said Robo receptor ectodomain D3 or D4 inhibits intracellular signaling via Robo receptor intracellular domain. 22. The agent of claim 21, wherein said inhibiting intracellular signaling via Robo receptor intracellular domain comprises inhibiting Robo recruitment of intracellular protein effectors. 23. The agent of claim 21 or 22, wherein said inhibiting intracellular signaling via Robo receptor intracellular domain is selected from the group consisting of inhibiting Robo receptor internalization, inhibiting Robo receptor phosphorylation and inhibiting Robo receptor depho sphorylation . 24. The agent of any one of claims 1-23, selected from the group consisting of an antibody, a peptide, a polypeptide, a peptidomimetic, a DNA, an RNA, an aptamer (DNA/RNA), a peptoid, a zDNA, a peptide nucleic acid (PNA), a locked nucleic acid (LNA), a lectin, an adnectin and a dendrimer. 25. The agent of any one of claims 1-24, wherein said Robo receptor ectodomain is a human Robo receptor ectodomain. 26. The agent of claim 25, wherein said human Robo receptor ectodomain is selected from the group consisting of a human Robol ectodomain, a human Robo 2 ectodomain and a human Robo 3 ectodomain. 27. The agent of claim 26, wherein said human Robo receptor ectodomain is a human Robo2 ectodomain. 28. A polynucleotide comprising a nucleic acid sequence encoding the agent of claim 24, wherein said agent is a peptide or polypeptide agent. 29. A pharmaceutical composition comprising the agent of any one of claims 1-27 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 30. The agent of any one of claims 1-19 for use in treating a Robo receptor associated condition or disorder. 31. The agent for use of claim 30, wherein said Robo receptor associated condition or disorder is a fibrotic condition or disorder. 32. The agent for use of claim 31, wherein said fibrotic disorder is selected from the group consisting of liver fibrosis and kidney fibrosis. 33. The agent for use of claim 30, wherein said Robo receptor associated condition or disorder is a cancer. 34. The agent for use of claim 33, wherein said cancer is tumor angiogenesis and/or metastatic cancer. 35. The agent for use of claim 30, wherein said Robo receptor associated condition or disorder is a neurological condition or disorder. 36. The agent for use of claim 30, wherein said Robo receptor associated condition or disorder is an orthopedic condition or disorder. 37. A method of identifying an agent for inhibiting Robo receptor activation comprising contacting candidate agents with a Robo receptor ectodomain polypeptide comprising a Robo receptor Immuno globulin-like domain 3 (D3), Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 4 (D4), a Robo receptor Immunoglobulin-like domain 5 (D5), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 1 (FnIII-l)(D6), a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 2 (FnIII-2)(D7) and a Robo receptor Fibronectin type III domain 3 (FnIII-3)(D8), or a portion thereof and selecting candidate agents capable of binding to said polypeptide or portion thereof with a sub-micromolar dissociation constant. 38. The method of claim 37, comprising selecting candidate agents binding to a Robo receptor ectodomain D4 dimerization interface with a sub-micromolar dissociation constant. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein said binding is characterized by a dissociation constant in the range of 10-250 nM. 40. The method of any one of claims 37-38, further comprising selecting candidate agents capable of inhibiting Robo receptor dimerization.
